title: Recurring motifs in EVA Q
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILER] Recurring motifs in EVA Q             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 04, 2012 9:05 pm So, part of the reason why Q left some of us in despair is because of its many deviations - not so much from the original series, we expected that, we wanted new content. What schocked so many of us was that it didn't look anything like the previous two Rebuild-movies. Many recurring motifs, art choices and details of the storytelling style were thrown right out of the window, allusions not followed, probably to make us feel like we missed 14 years, too, to destroy the familiar setting some crazies like to hide in, because they can't even find the EVA-setting in EVA. No more staircase poster, no more pen pen, no more ending the moviers with a handhold, no more "Beautiful World" - even the paratext seems to be mocking us. The train scenes that both movies added more of? Gone. The isolation motif, that even Mari got buildup for? Bye-Bye. The keepsake motif? The only one remaining is the SDAT-player, (Doll? Glasses? Cross neckackle?) and that's only serving the same purpose it has served since day one, really. Even freakin' EVA 01 is gone. ...This, however, makes the few remaining motifs that have recurred through these movies AND lived into Q all the more significant, as they may or may not actually allow us to make predictions about final... Q should have proven that Rebuild is most definitely NOT just mindless fanservice and deserves to be taken appart with the same dilligence as the original. In other words: Let's chew down on the symbolism. I The First Name motif SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 04, 2012 9:12 pm My favorite: the disembodied heads motif! Eva-08 shoots off Mark.09's head Lilith's head has been severed from her body Disembodied heads of the Ayanami Series are kept in a wall at Nerv HQ The floor of deepest Central Dogma is littered with countless giant human-like skulls Mark.09 takes off Mark.06's head with a sickle Eva-02 bites off Mark.09's regrown head Kaworu's head goes *splat* all over the entry plug viewscreen Eva was never a stranger to decapitations, but this installment really goes crazy with it...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 04, 2012 9:23 pm ^I suppose the headless Mark 9 is somehow supposed to represent how it eventually gets used by SEELE, or how the chick piloting it at first seems to lack the qualities generally associated with a head, a will of her own. (That and to show how cool Mari is by letting her score an instant headshot. Too bad the thing's indesctructible) Maybe it all represents loss of will/ability to decide/control over themselves. I think it's less the heads themselves than it is the act of beheading. But you're right. That's too often to be coincididence/simply schock factor. Rei's scythe is, of course, also rather blatant in its meaning, as are the plants breaking through the concrete at NERv HQ where Kaworu has his piano. Another thing is the sentence "Did I save you/her?" in its endless repetitions as arc words of sort. Interesting because, in episode 23, it was Rei asking if she'd saved Shinji. Go ahead and count just how often he says this or some variation of this. The answer is, of course,no. Rei II's gone, but her presence still hangs heavily over this movies, doesn't it? To gwen stacy levels. Kaworu's talk also contunes that change motif from 2.0 at various points. "In time, even your current self will be overwritten" (ie, become an adult...?) - It was once supposed to be associated with Mari, wasn't it? Not seeing much of that, but then there IS her role in the ending battle and yet another attempt to talk sense into Shinji, obvious echo there.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Tue Dec 04, 2012 9:32 pm Shinji being the stars and Rei being the moon is an interesting take on things. It also makes me think this. The stars are seemingly constant and unchanging. But they do change. It's just slower. And when it happens it's more major. Their posistions change. And when a star goes nova or otherwise really changes it affects their entire solar system more than anything else could. I think Mari eating in her entry plug and making it look like a room or a car is another nice reminder about her charecter. Mari is someone one who's comfortable right from the get go with being an Eva pilot. She's comforatble in Eva. She actually seems to straight up be comfortable about herself. That's something you can't say about the other pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 04, 2012 10:09 pm Darkwing wrote: Shinji being the stars and Rei being the moon is an interesting take on things. It also makes me think this. The stars are seemingly constant and unchanging. But they do change. It's just slower. And when it happens it's more major. Their posistions change. And when a star goes nova or otherwise really changes it affects their entire solar system more than anything else could.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Tue Dec 04, 2012 10:24 pm Well don't read too much into it. That was just some fooling around on my part.

[]:             Quote Post by kuwisdelu » Tue Dec 04, 2012 10:26 pm Something that just occurred to me: if we'd had a train sequence in Q, which Rei would have been in it?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 04, 2012 10:36 pm kuwisdelu wrote: Something that just occurred to me: if we'd had a train sequence in Q, which Rei would have been in it?

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Tue Dec 04, 2012 10:56 pm Another one is Diecide. Men keep finding or creating gods, and keep killing them and dissecting them, each time trying to make one more fitting to their desires.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Dec 05, 2012 1:49 pm I think it's more like humanity outgrowing the gods. Interestingly, both Gendo AND Misato sprout such talk about defying destiny, only SEELE still follows it/wants to bring it about, even if Gendo and Misato probably have different ideas there - Misato probably just wants all the magic alien stuff out of the world. Gendo wants to use it, but not as intended.

[]:             Quote Post by Shakabutt » Wed Dec 05, 2012 2:21 pm edit: yeah, i fail at symbolism :)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Dec 05, 2012 2:54 pm ^Celestial body symbolism often has more to do with the cultural perception of these shiny things in the sky than their actual physical position, dude. The assosiations themselves are clearly present.

[]:             Quote Post by enact » Mon Jan 14, 2013 8:19 am Darkwing wrote: Shinji being the stars and Rei being the moon is an interesting take on things. It also makes me think this. The stars are seemingly constant and unchanging. But they do change. It's just slower. And when it happens it's more major. Their posistions change. And when a star goes nova or otherwise really changes it affects their entire solar system more than anything else could.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 8:37 am There are lots of visual reminders from EoE. Lilith's severed head, the red ocean, and the Moon with loads of red cuts to name just a few.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Tue Jan 15, 2013 6:04 am Mari doesnt have snack food, she has liquid sustanances, like those astronauts use, it seems liquid but it solid paste, how else were they to eat inside a liquid enviroment.

[]:             Quote Post by Mt Olympus » Thu Jan 17, 2013 4:47 am I believe there are a lot of recurring themes in 3.0. Most of them being along the lines of a macro/micro type and opposites/reflections. We begin the movie 14 yrs in the future, much like when the 1.0 starts when Shinji is 14. This is obvious. His first major interaction is with Misato, however this and many other recurrences are opposites. Instead of the heartwarming, comfortable Misato we are given a much much colder version of her, like how Gendo is to Shinji. Also the roles Misato and Ritsuko are similar to what Gendo and Fuyutsuki play. 3.0 starts with an angel battle. Instead of Nerv fighting it's Wille. Instead of Shinji being reluctant to pilot Eva 01 he pretty much begs to. And is told he will never do anything again which is pretty opposite to what being offered to pilot Eva 01 was. As a viewer we are left completely oblivious to what is actually going on, much like how the show starts. We are pretty much left forced into Shinji's shoes. The list can go on and on. Some of the relation 3.0 has is, like I've mentioned, opposites. An example would be the 4th impact being done to change the world and not for "selfish" reasons. Also, it feels like 1.0 and 2.0 were squashed into 3.0. Macro/micro and math. OK, one more thing. There's a lot of one thing containing two things going on or just fractals of another thing.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Thu Jan 17, 2013 11:37 am Mt Olympus wrote: His first major interaction is with Misato, however this and many other recurrences are opposites. Instead of the heartwarming, comfortable Misato we are given a much much colder version of her, like how Gendo is to Shinji. Also the roles Misato and Ritsuko are similar to what Gendo and Fuyutsuki play.

[]:             Quote Post by Mt Olympus » Thu Jan 17, 2013 9:25 pm K40s wrote: his first major interaction is with Toji's sister who fills the role of the kind Misato driving him to see his father after so many years, instead he meets a Gendo-like Misato after many years of being trapped in eva-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Jan 18, 2013 5:47 am Yeah, there are lots of parallels with the beginning of the TV series and 1.0. For example there's the scene where he encounters Eva-13; he sees a purple Eva, and Gendo formally introduces it while standing above him. Then there's the scene where he goes looking for Rei, and, just like in the original series and 1.0, he finds her naked in her room, she proceeds to dress herself, and he tries to talk to her while she ignores him. Gendo and Misato are much more similar in 3.0; they even both have a fixation with concealing their eyes (Gendo's Keel visor and Misato's cap+sunglasses combo). Sakura has been thought of as a "mini-Misato" since the beginning, so I'm not surprised she's taking that role now. The usage of Ode to Joy during 4th Impact also brought back lots of memories of Episode 24. Although I thought it would have been better used during the battle between Eva-13 and Eva-02 (which also is similar to the battle between Eva-01 and Eva-02 in Episode 24). There are references everywhere you look...

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Jan 18, 2013 8:20 am Here's one Rebuild-specific motif that definitely carries over: Evas with four arms! In 2.0, it's of course first seen when Unit-03 becomes possessed -- in Q, we see it when another Eva is invested with two souls. Is Kaworu the Angel "possessing" Shinji? Or maybe, when Shinji insists on retrieving the spears, it's the other way around.
